free_text: Isotopic dependence of the ion-ion potential in the
Details of experiment
- Elastic
scattering of 16O by even mass nickel at 38-56 MeV
- Inelastic
scattering of 16O by even mass nickel at 36-56 MeV
The Ni-targets, thickness about 30 mug/cm**2, with a
thin layer of gold with the aim of data normalization.
The Ni-58,60,62,64 targets were 99.9, 99.9, 98.4 and
96.7% enriched, respectively.
Coupled channel analysis. The double-folding
method.
A set of nine surface barrier detectors.
The Sao Paulo 8UD pelletron accelerator.
- By authors. Due to the high precision required for
the experimental data, the following cares were taken
into account in the data acquisition and reduction-
the use of a monitor detector to be sure that no target
deterioration occurred during the bombardment.
High-energy resolution to allow a complete separation
between the elastic and inelastic(2+) peaks and
separate the elastic and inelastic peaks from the other
elastic peaks associated with the contaminants in the
targets.
No information on sources of uncertainty
Data tables were received from IAEA NDS.
Data tables were received from IAEA NDS.
All data were changed by tabular data.
Naohiko's remark is included.
Last checking has been done.
SD: Small correction in REFERENCE. Data at
Ep=38 MeV were deleted - data from another article (see
REL-REF). Ref. NP/A,656,187,1999 deleted from REL-REF
(not relevant to this experiment). ERR-T -> DATA-ERR.
The zeros to right side in values were added to
relevant values.
